Hey guys and welcome to the Coach Mining and Enrollment Extractor Workbook. Now this is probably my favorite way to get insights on my audience and really develop the hooks that are going to serve as a foundation for my marketing later, especially my sales pages and my emails. So just as a little background, this is a process I started about a year ago. I would say and I started it because I realized that a lot of the clients I was working with were really successful in their sales conversations and their enrollment conversations. So I wanted to see what was going on there specifically. So I would start requesting to listen to recordings of their sales calls and I would have them transcribed and I would listen to them very attentively and just highlight in the transcripts anything that really stood out, especially those aha moments, those shifts in the prospect's mind that all of a sudden opened them up to a new level of responsiveness and receptivity. So essentially listening and then reading later in the transcript how my clients were coaching the conversion in real time on a sales call and then extracting those insights and being able to coach the same conversion at scale. So that's the background of it. And I would suggest taking about three or five of these enrollment or strategy calls if you're working on SaaS, you could even do this on your product demos and just really going through them carefully and noticing where things shift. What is your audience revealing? And as a side benefit of this, when you listen to your client in that environment, in that selling context, in that coaching the conversion context, that is where you get to get a really solid feel and grasp of their communication style and their voice and their languaging patterns and could channel that into your copy later. So I don't do anything very intentionally to try to mimic a client's voice. In fact, I do warn them that their voice can and will shift when I start writing for them. But this is one of those things where I feel like I can embody them even more fully when I'm listening to them communicate in a sales context right before writing myself. So that's a little side note. If you don't currently have any sales or enrollment calls to draw from, I would highly, highly recommend doing what it takes to get some. So you could have your client or yourself send an email to your list. You can create a Facebook post on your page, just opening up the line for any Q&A or strategy call. You can host an AMA on Facebook live, whatever it takes to really just get that one-on-one conversation where you could go through the questions in this workbook and extract those insights that we're going to use to coach the conversion on your sales page. So that is the gist of this exercise. You will find throughout this workbook. There is a question bank. So these aren't questions that are meant to be asked in any particular order, although the structure, the sections are relevant and typically how you would want to be running a sales or enrollment call anyway. But this by no means is a linear, first-has number one, the number two, the number three, the number four, the number five. These are just suggestions that elicit the type of responses we're looking for. The first section is all about the questions used to discover that moment of highest tension. If you are a coach, a lot of these will be familiar. What specifically is happening in your life right now that made you take this call? There's obviously a level of intent and motivation. There's something active that they're experiencing with that wound, with that moment of highest tension. We want to know exactly what that is. We want to know about how it felt last time that problem happened. We're really dimensionalizing it, getting those specific details of how that is affecting all the other stakeholders in their lives. We want to know obviously what they've been doing to try to overcome it or achieve that outcome, how that's working out. That's going to reveal some shortcomings around either the power times or mindsets or the sacred cows or their competitors, just all very important juicy stuff that we can position against later. Of course, we want to know why they want your help, why now. That's going to give you your perceived USP. What does the market currently see in you and your ability to solve it, to motivate them to take that step with you? One way of asking that, I'm humbled by the opportunity to help you today and the commitment you've made to overcoming that problem, just so I'm aware, what was the tipping point or catalyst that led to that decision? We want to know that tipping point because that is going to be the clearest indicator of what that moment of highest tension is. Moving on to discover that moment of highest pleasure, this will often also come up naturally in that enrollment conversation, but I do suggest asking one if not more of these questions to really open it up in a powerful way. Very famous question, I think credit goes to Dan Sullivan of Strategic Co-Chair, but if we're having this conversation X months or years in the future, looking back, what must have happened personally, professionally, financially for you to say it's been the best year and decision of your life? Where do they feel redeemed? Where are they comfortable having that whiskey clinker, that champagne clink, which we spoke about earlier, and reflecting on the year that was? What happened? Of course, we have the magic wand question, pretty famous question over there. I like this one, what do you want to be most proud of? That really gets them to this place of just this mindset of reflecting on what has happened in a way that is emotionally stirring for them. That's what we're trying to do, we're really trying to stir up those emotions, not just attack it intellectually. If you could literally, if you literally couldn't fail while pursuing X, what would your life look like X months from now? This is getting them out of the objection and problem mindset and really just focused on that image of that moment of highest pleasure. Who would you be most excited to share that win with? Very important, once again, adding dimensionality to that moment of highest pleasure. Who in your life would benefit most from you achieving that outcome? I like this one, what's your favorite drink meal placed in the world? If we were sitting across from each other clinking that drink in X months from now, what would we be toasting to? It's a very tactile, evocative, imaginary exercise. It's a visualization they could easily place themselves in. It just brings to surface that tangible result, the internal, emotional result of it, and this overflowing sense of fulfillment and pride around it. The last question here, who did you have to become in order for that new reality to happen? This is just acknowledging that a shift has to occur. Our marketing is designed to create that transformation, get them from that place of point A to point buyer. That is the first transformation and of course the transformations that follow once they are engaging in your product and trying to engineer that new reality. Just getting them into the mindset that yes, transformations have to occur, shifts have to occur in order for those new realities to come to fruition. Next section is all about discovering those sacred cows and engineering the new beliefs. Very simple question, why haven't you achieved what you want to achieve? Or that moment of highest pleasure? How long have you been actively pursuing it? Why has it been taking so long? What do you believe? I love this question because it just triggers this sense of, in a way, it's urgency, but in a way, it also elicits that sense of envy. If other people have done it, A, it creates the possibility that it can be done and B, it creates a sense of why not me. So, what do you believe the people who have achieved X or overcome Y have that you don't? What do you think made them successful? What makes them special? Here you'll start to identify the limiting beliefs they have around solving that problem. Of course, that's a sacred cow that you're going to be able to reframe in your marketing or in this conversation. Why do you think that's happening? That's a follow-up question just to dive deeper into the root of it, acknowledging and accepting that many people in your exact situation, and you've got to use that phrase, exact situation have gone on to overcome X or achieve Y, why you believe you've yet to join them in that success of just positioning that possibility and creating that sense of envy. I should be able to do that too. What is that perceived injustice aspect that we've been speaking about throughout? You don't want it to be this aspirational thing of what I could do. You want it to be what should be. And it definitely amplifies that when they see that other people who have been in their situation are getting that result. That creates a should. What do you think others have had to overcome to get that desirable outcome? So if they feel really stuck in disillusion in their past, it's easier to frame this question in terms of others. What have others overcome to get that? And then they'll start seeing that they could do and inviting that resourcefulness to do it as well. What positive and even scary shifts do you think others have had to make to get that outcome? So this is just a follow-up to number six. Moving on, bridge over troubled water. I just love using some in ungarfunkel references. But what I'm saying here is essentially what they need to do to start seeing you as that bridge. So you'll get into this part of the conversation after you've uncovered the moment of highest tension and the moment of highest pleasure as well as those sacred cows. So what would it take for us to start moving towards that together? If we were to end this call right now, do you feel you'd be able to confidently and efficiently move towards that moment of highest pleasure on your own? Would you make it? Why or why not? And what concerns you most about your path towards the moment of highest pleasure? And then my favorite, acknowledging that this is possible and that many others have succeeded in achieving it. What do you personally need right now to start taking powerful strides in that direction? So that's for the bridge over troubled water. It's going to have a good correlation with the blue ocean bridge part of your sales page. And then finally, the Mrs. Robinson, yes, another Simon and Garfunkel reference. And this is just taking that further beyond the bridge. This is about you as the person who is leading them across that bridge. So if I were to guide you every step of the way, would you feel safe committing to that today? Are you willing to accept my help to get there? How can I help you right now in this moment to start moving towards that moment of highest pleasure? How much faster do you think you'll achieve it if we work on it together? So these are questions I will be very relevant and useful if you are running sales conversations, enrollment conversations, which is of course a little bit beyond the scope of this program, but by all means use this as a resource for that. But you do want to see their responses here. And this will often bring up some objections that you're going to be able to counter in your upgraded FAQ clothes or your perfect for you if clothes. So that's the Mrs. Robinson. We have a few more questions here that will elicit information for that perfect for you if as well as sources of exclusive empowerment, which we spoke about before. What do you feel you've experienced in the past that makes you ready to get results working with me? So this is where they get to channel all those points of pride and cash in on those points. In what ways do you feel like your previous setbacks have prepared you for this moment? So this is where they get to alchemize all the pain before and turn that into their biggest opportunity. There's a lot of Victor Frankel reframing there, taking those setbacks, those negative things and using that as the building blocks for a better future. Another resource on that would be the markets search for meaning theme plate inside six figure emails. Highly recommend you check that out. What part of our possible work together excites you the most and what part of our work together scares you the most? There should be an element of fear. There is transformation. There is a shift happening and you want to know where those little hesitations may be so you can counter them on the call and of course empower them on the sales page when you're doing this at scale. Risk mitigator close a few questions here. What do you know for certain will happen if nothing were to change? What do you feel is at stake if nothing changes knowing that staying where you are is a choice that's important phrasing. Do you feel comfortable making it why or why not? When you really tune in, does the excitement of pursuing X outcome with me outweigh the risk discomfort fear of staying where you are? All these questions will bring forth really great insights for the risk mitigator close of your sales page. A few more things for the upgraded FAQ. Very simple. What questions do you have before moving forward together? Do you have any questions about partnering together to generate that moment of highest pleasure? Then one bonus assignment which yields tremendous insights every time I do it is called the sound of silence. You're going to do this when you're re-listening to those sales calls later. If you are getting them transcribed but I recommend you do ask the transcriber or the service to clearly list out silence wherever there's a pause of more than a few seconds because it's really in those pauses that there is a new insight on their end that something is not computing with their current view of things and something has shifted dramatically and it's those aha moments, those little epiphanies that really create the newfound resourcefulness to pursue an opportunity. We want to highlight those, identify those whenever we can and then unpack them a little. What happened to cause that pause? What question preceded that silence? What do you think is going on in your prospects head during that silence? If you could actually script their internal dialogue, what would that look like? Then of course we want to narrow that down to the aha moment or insight that just shifted for them. This is the belief that dissolved in that silence and what new belief or sense of possibility is filling up that vacuum. With all that said, this is the coach mining enrollment extractor. It is definitely my favorite way of gathering rich insight, channeling my client's voice and if they have an existing bank of enrollment calls or sales calls right now, this is something I highly recommend you do right away. If they don't, I suggest you get a few calls on the calendar as soon as possible because the insights that this yields could be your research in of itself. But all that said, take a breather, plan how you are going to best leverage this and I'll see you in the next lesson.
